description: Letter, fragmentary and calligraphic, regarding business. Mentionins dealings in corals, storax, and zaituni (silk), and merchants of the second half of the eleventh century (Joseph b. Farah Qabisi). On verso is a medical prescription in Arabic letters. Most likely the receiver of the business letter was a physician. Information from Goitein's note card. Possibly the same scribe as T-S NS J194.
tags: merchant, Arabic script, silk, physician, prescription, business
description_he: מכתב, מקוטע וקליגרפי, בנושאים עסקיים; עוסק באלמוג, שרף וזיתוני (משי); מוזכר יוסף בן פרח קביסי; גב: מרשם רפואי בכתב ערבי
